Package org.apache.juneau.petstore.auth

Class StubBearerTokenValidator

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.petstore.auth.StubBearerTokenValidator
All Implemented Interfaces:
TokenValidator
public class StubBearerTokenValidator extends Object implements TokenValidator
Stub TokenValidator for the petstore demo — recognises an in-memory map of opaque bearer tokens.

NOT FOR PRODUCTION USE. This is a sample/demo validator that maps fixed token strings to fixed user principals. Real deployments should plug in JwtTokenValidator from juneau-rest-server-auth-jwt (signed JWTs against a JWKS), an opaque-token store backed by a database, or RFC 7662 introspection.

Default token table:

  • petstore-user → principal "alice"
  • petstore-admin → principal "admin"

Other token strings throw AuthenticationException carrying a WWW-Authenticate: Bearer realm="petstore" challenge — the AuthFilterChain in turn returns 401.

  • Method Details

    • validate

      public Principal validate(String token) throws AuthenticationException
      Description copied from interface: TokenValidator
      Validates the supplied bearer token and returns the associated Principal.
      Specified by:
      validate in interface TokenValidator
      Parameters:
      token - The raw token string from Authorization: Bearer <token>. Never null.
      Returns:
      The authenticated principal. Must not be null on success.
      Throws:
      AuthenticationException - If the token is invalid, expired, revoked, or otherwise unacceptable.
